task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into Moodle as admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Moodle interface is visible in a web browser with the user logged in as 'Admin User' (typically visible in the top right corner).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the BIO101 course question bank.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Breadcrumbs or page header shows 'Introduction to Biology' or 'BIO101', and a Question bank tab (Questions, Categories, Import, or Export) is active.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Pharmacology Module 3' category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Categories' tab in the Question bank shows 'Pharmacology Module 3' in the list of course categories.

task_completion.description: Verify the category description matches the task requirement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Drug classifications, mechanisms, and clinical applications' is visible either under the category name in the category list or within the category edit form.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent imported the GIFT file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An import success screen showing 'Importing 8 questions from file' or the file 'pharmacology_questions.gift' visible in the file upload area of the Import tab with 'GIFT format' selected.

task_completion.description: Verify the 8 specific questions are present in the new category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Question bank 'Questions' tab shows the category 'Pharmacology Module 3' selected, and the list contains the expected questions (e.g., 'Penicillin Mechanism', 'Beta-Blocker Target', 'Warfarin Antidote', etc.).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser GUI to interact with Moodle.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) navigating the Moodle web interface, not terminal curl commands or direct database scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent uploaded the provided GIFT file rather than manually typing questions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Moodle file picker or drag-and-drop interface should show 'pharmacology_questions.gift' being uploaded, or the import screen should be visible.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the Moodle application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show SQL insert commands, mysql, sqlite3, or moodle CLI scripts being used to create the category or insert questions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Course: BIO101. Target Category: 'Pharmacology Module 3'. Expected Category Description: 'Drug classifications, mechanisms, and clinical applications'. GIFT File Path: '/home/ga/Documents/pharmacology_questions.gift'. Expected Question Count: 8. Expected Question Names: 'Penicillin Mechanism', 'Beta-Blocker Target', 'Warfarin Antidote', 'ACE Inhibitor Side Effect', 'Insulin Storage', 'Digoxin Toxicity Sign', 'Nitroglycerin Administration', 'Heparin Monitoring'. Correct answer for 'Warfarin Antidote' is 'Vitamin K (phytonadione)'. Correct answer for 'Heparin Monitoring' is 'Activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT)'.
